visual: Arrays with Pictures (dot mode): 2×5 dot array (returns ). Equation Builder in expression mode.
guide: "You just described this array by ROWS: 2 rows of 5. That gave us 2 times 5."
visual_2: System highlights rows briefly. Expression `2 × 5` appears in Equation Builder, then fades.
guide_2: "But watch what happens when I look at the COLUMNS instead."
visual_3: System highlights column 1 (2 dots going up and down), then column 2, column 3, column 4, column 5 sequentially.
guide_3: "I see 5 columns. Each column has 2 dots. So I can also say: 5 columns of 2."
guide_4: "5 columns of 2 becomes 5 times 2. The first number is how many columns. The second number is how many in each column."
guide_5: "Same array, but a different description which gives us  a different expression. 2 rows of 5 gave us 2 times 5. 5 columns of 2 gives us 5 times 2. Both describe THIS array."
visual_5: Both expressions display briefly: `2 × 5` and `5 × 2` with the same dot array between them.
guide_6: "The rule is: your DESCRIPTION determines your EXPRESSION. What you SAY about the array is what you WRITE. Let's try one."
visual_6: Equation Builder clears. New 4×3 dot array appears. Word description active: ___ columns of ___. Tile palette: 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9.
guide_7: "This array has 4 rows of 3. But what's the column description? Count the columns going up and down, then count how many in each column."
student_action_step_1: Word description template: "___ columns of ___". Student fills two number slots.
guide_8: "3 columns of 4. Now build the expression to match."
visual_7: Same array. Same tile palette: 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9. "3 columns of 4" remains. Equation Builder slots appear ____ `×` ___
on_correct: "3 columns of 4. 3 times 4. The description matches the expression."
